title: International Developed Markets Analysis - Client 033
content: International developed markets offer valuation advantages, currency diversification, and exposure to global industry leaders often unavailable in US markets. Regional Overview: European markets trading at 30% discount to US equities on P/E basis. Japanese companies benefit from corporate governance reforms and shareholder-friendly policies. Pacific markets (Australia, New Zealand) provide commodity exposure and stable dividend yields. Key Investment Themes: European luxury goods brands (LVMH, Hermès) with pricing power and emerging market demand. Japanese automotive and robotics leaders (Toyota, Fanuc) pioneering electric vehicles and automation. Swiss pharmaceuticals (Novartis, Roche) with diverse drug pipelines. German industrial champions (Siemens, SAP) leading Industry 4.0 transformation. Currency Considerations: Unhedged approach provides natural diversification. Dollar strength creates attractive entry points for international investments. Euro, yen, and pound exposure reduces single-currency dependency. Portfolio Construction: For client-033's $2.1M international allocation, recommend 40% European equities via region-specific ETFs (VGK, FEZ), 30% Japanese exposure through both broad market (EWJ) and focused funds (DBJP for quality), 20% Pacific region (EWA for Australia), 10% developed markets dividend aristocrats (VIGI). Avoid emerging market bleed-through in developed indices. Risk Management: ADR vehicles provide US-listed access reducing settlement risks. Diversification across 20+ countries and 100+ companies mitigates single-country exposure. Expected returns: 8-12% annually with favorable valuations supporting outperformance versus US markets in coming years.
